reasoning: Based on searches from 2025, DeepSeek R1 and V3 were highly competitive (released late 2024/early 2025), briefly scoring near the top or leading open-source models. However, proprietary giants like Google (Gemini 2.5 Pro), xAI (Grok 3), and OpenAI have consistently launched models that take the #1 overall spot on Chatbot Arena. While DeepSeek might release a new model (like V4 or R2) around December 2025 given their past cadence, it would need to release early enough in the month to accumulate enough battles, establish a public Elo score, and convincingly beat whatever frontier models the US tech giants have active at that moment (like Claude 4, GPT-5, or Grok 3). The fierce competition among 5-6 top players makes the chance of any single company holding the #1 spot precisely on December 31 relatively low, and DeepSeek, as an open-source/open-weight contender, historically sits slightly below the absolute #1 spot after the initial hype cycle. Therefore, I estimate a ~9% chance.
